channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: Something for the Bank Holiday Weekend – 02/05/26
item.content:encoded:
The traditionally damp bank holiday weather is coming for the UK, but that won’t stop many from ill-advised barbecues… or at least it won’t stop advertisers from convincing us that everyone in the UK tries to barbecue under a brolly in the pouring rain. Whatever your dinner plans, it’s likely a better weekend for catching up on games once the clouds roll in. I plan on more runs of Saros.

In the News This Week

  • Subnautica 2 Early Access release date set for May 14th
  • Star Wars: Galactic Racer is going podracing in October
  • The Steam Controller launches this May for £85
  • Greedfall developer Spiders is being shut down
  • The Division Resurgence is now playable on PC – season roadmap revealed
  • The Nacon Connect stream has been rescheduled for 7th May
  • The Outer Worlds: Spacer’s Choice Edition gets major updates and becomes a free upgrade
  • Atomfall is being turned into a TV series
  • Thick as Thieves is cheap as chips, and it’s coming out in May
  • Final Fantasy VII Rebirth demo has dropped on Switch 2 & Xbox

Games in Review & Featured Articles

We had a bunch of interesting titles in for review this week. An excellent new Survivors-like and a retro-styled horror game top the table this time.

  • TerraTech Legion – PS5, XSX|S, PC – 9/10
  • Ground Zero – PS5, XSX|S, PC – 9/10
  • Moomintroll: Winter’s Warmth – NSW, PC – 8/10
  • Adorable Adventures – PS5, XSX|S, PC – 7/10
  • The Spell Brigade – PS5, PC – 7/10
  • Tides of Tomorrow – PS5, XSX|S, PC – 6/10
  • Aphelion – PS5, XSX|S, PC – 6/10

But this week also had a bunch of major previews. I got to go hands on with 007: First Light, and I was pleasantly surprised at how IO Interactive has remixed their classic Hitman gameplay for the different style of stealth action. A similar style of reinvention is coming to Supermassive’s horror series with Directive 8020. I also checked out Final Fantasy VII Rebirth on Switch 2, which is a pleasant surprise.

Jason, meanwhile, previewed Far Far West, which brings lashings of Helldivers 2-style co-op action to the robot Western setting, and Dom enjoyed his time with The Player Who Can’t Level Up, which does actually include a lot of levelling up.

Finishing up for the week, What We Played featured Tides of Tomorrow, TerraTech Legion & Adorable Adventures.

Trailer Park

Remedy share a new Control Resonant dev diary

Check out the trailer for the Resident Evil film reboot

A bunch of Star Wars experiences are jumping into Fortnite today

That’s the round up for this week. Hope you have a great weekend ahead and we’ll see you back here in earnest on Tuesday!



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/05/02/something-for-the-bank-holiday-weekend-02-05-26/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
